Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka (To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|12=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=draft}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=draft}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=draft}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|12=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=draft}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=draft}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=draft}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|12=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=draft|12=draft}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft|12=draft}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=draft|12=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft|12=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|12=draft|12=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}}

revision.comment: 37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:錦織神社 up to rev 96879511
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|大阪府富田林市にある神社|その他}} {{神社 |名称 = 錦織神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
本殿（中央）と摂社（両側） |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町9-46 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 43.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 19.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[スサノオ|建速素戔嗚命]]
[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳（[[平安時代]]中期か） |本殿 = 錦織造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 秋期大祭（10月第2土曜日） |神事 = }} [[File:錦織神社の本殿 富田林市宮甲田町 2012.3.15 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿（重要文化財）]] '''錦織神社'''（にしこおりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。地元では錦織を「にしこり」「にしこおり」「にしごり」等と呼んでいる。当社は[[建水分神社]]（上水分社、[[千早赤阪村]]）、[[美具久留御魂神社]]（下水分社、富田林市）とともに「[[河内国]]の三水分（みくまり）社」と呼ばれている。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|建速素戔嗚命]]（たけはやすさのおのみこと）、[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[菅原道真]] == 歴史 == 当社のある地域は、太古は「'''錦部'''（にしごり）'''部'''」と呼ばれていたようで、織物の技術を持つ人々が[[百済]]より渡来し住みついていた場所とされている。当社はそもそもの名称を水郡（にごり）神社といい、錦部部（錦部氏）の[[氏神]]であった。 創建年代は不明であるが、[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の本殿修理の際に地中から[[平安時代]]中期の丸瓦・平瓦が発見され、その頃かそれ以前に創建されたと推定されている[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/　錦織神社 | 日光東照宮のデザインに影響を与えた神社 ～ 富田林市 錦織神社 御朱印 アクセス～ - ミナカワ]2019年2月14日　閲覧。また、爾吾利天王（にごりてんのう）、水郡天王宮とも呼ばれていた。平安時代には[[神宮寺]]として金輪寺が建立され、[[神仏習合]]が行われていた。 当社は[[大和川]]支流である[[石川 (大阪府)|石川]]の谷に位置することから、[[千早赤阪村]]の[[建水分神社]]や[[富田林市]]宮町にある[[美具久留御魂神社]]とともに、古くから[[河内国]]の三水分（みくまり）社として広く信仰を集めていた[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html　錦織神社 - 富田林市文化財課]2019年2月14日　閲覧。 [[重要文化財]]である本殿は、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]18年（[[1363年]]）に三善貞行によって再建されたものである。 [[明治]]時代になると[[神仏分離]]、[[廃仏毀釈]]が行われて神宮寺の金輪寺が廃寺となった。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[郷社]]に列せられている。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された際に錦織神社に改称している。 == 境内 == [[Image:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|参道前の鳥居
（この写真の左側に[[富田林市立川西小学校|川西小学校]]の門がある）]] [[富田林市立川西小学校|川西小学校]]の門の横に大石鳥居があり、そこから約150メートルの長い参道が続いている。長い参道はかつて[[流鏑馬]]が行われており馬場先と呼ばれていた。 * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[正平 (日本)|正平]]18年（[[1363年]]）に三善貞行により再建。入母屋造三間社、正面千鳥破風、軒唐破風付き、檜皮葺きとする。正面の向拝には丸みを帯びた唐破風を造り、屋根の斜面正面に付けられた三角形の千鳥破風が唐破風の上に位置する。こうした屋根形式は[[室町時代]]の[[神社建築]]としては珍しく、貴重なものとされる。この建築様式は「'''錦織造'''」と呼ばれる複雑な屋根様式の原型であり、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]に建築された神社に多く採用された。また、[[日光東照宮]]にも影響を与えたといわれている。 * 神門 * 祈祷所 * 神輿庫 * 拝殿（割拝殿） * 社務所 * 遙拝所 * [[天誅組]]記念碑 - 参道脇に天誅組記念碑が設置されている。これは幕末当時に甲田村の大庄屋であった[[水郡善之祐]]が[[天誅組の変]]において、天誅組河内勢の中心人物として息子の英太郎とともに決起したことを示すものである。決起から100年後の[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に水郡家の子孫らによって建立された。 == 摂末社 == === 摂社 === * 天神社（重要文化財） - 祭神：[[カグツチ|火産霊神]]、[[大国主]]神、[[えびす|恵比寿神]]。二間社流見世棚造。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]12年（[[1480年]]）建立[http://archives.bunkenkyo.or.jp/national/nisigoori/index.html 文化遺産オンライン]、2009年3月8日閲覧。 * 春日社（重要文化財） - 祭神：[[春日神|春日大明神]]、[[熊野権現|熊野速玉神]]。二間社流見世棚造。文明12年（1480年）頃建立。 === 末社 === * [[金刀比羅宮]] * [[弁才天]]社 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 錦織神社 3棟 ** 本殿 ** 摂社天神社本殿 - 文明12年（1480年）の棟札がある。 ** 摂社春日社本殿 == 例祭 == * 秋期大祭（10月第2土曜日） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町9-46 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄長野線]][[川西駅 (大阪府)|川西駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（西へ約200m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html 錦織神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm 「錦織（にしごり）」のページ] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にしきおりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

本殿（中央）と摂社（両側） |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町9-46 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 43.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 19.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[スサノオ|建速素戔嗚命]]

[[菅原道真]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳（[[平安時代]]中期か） |本殿 = 錦織造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 秋期大祭（10月第2土曜日） |神事 = }} [[File:錦織神社の本殿 富田林市宮甲田町 2012.3.15 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿（重要文化財）]] '''錦織神社'''（にしこおりじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。地元では錦織を「にしこり」「にしこおり」「にしごり」等と呼んでいる。当社は[[建水分神社]]（上水分社、[[千早赤阪村]]）、[[美具久留御魂神社]]（下水分社、富田林市）とともに「[[河内国]]の三水分（みくまり）社」と呼ばれている。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|建速素戔嗚命]]（たけはやすさのおのみこと）、[[応神天皇|品陀別命]]、[[菅原道真]] == 歴史 == 当社のある地域は、太古は「'''錦部'''（にしごり）'''部'''」と呼ばれていたようで、織物の技術を持つ人々が[[百済]]より渡来し住みついていた場所とされている。当社はそもそもの名称を水郡（にごり）神社といい、錦部部（錦部氏）の[[氏神]]であった。 創建年代は不明であるが、[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の本殿修理の際に地中から[[平安時代]]中期の丸瓦・平瓦が発見され、その頃かそれ以前に創建されたと推定されている[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/　錦織神社 | 日光東照宮のデザインに影響を与えた神社 ～ 富田林市 錦織神社 御朱印 アクセス～ - ミナカワ]2019年2月14日　閲覧。また、爾吾利天王（にごりてんのう）、水郡天王宮とも呼ばれていた。平安時代には[[神宮寺]]として金輪寺が建立され、[[神仏習合]]が行われていた。 当社は[[大和川]]支流である[[石川 (大阪府)|石川]]の谷に位置することから、[[千早赤阪村]]の[[建水分神社]]や[[富田林市]]宮町にある[[美具久留御魂神社]]とともに、古くから[[河内国]]の三水分（みくまり）社として広く信仰を集めていた[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html　錦織神社 - 富田林市文化財課]2019年2月14日　閲覧。 [[重要文化財]]である本殿は、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]18年（[[1363年]]）に三善貞行によって再建されたものである。 [[明治]]時代になると[[神仏分離]]、[[廃仏毀釈]]が行われて神宮寺の金輪寺が廃寺となった。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[郷社]]に列せられている。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された際に錦織神社に改称している。 == 境内 == [[Image:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|参道前の鳥居
（この写真の左側に[[富田林市立川西小学校|川西小学校]]の門がある）]] [[富田林市立川西小学校|川西小学校]]の門の横に大石鳥居があり、そこから約150メートルの長い参道が続いている。長い参道はかつて[[流鏑馬]]が行われており馬場先と呼ばれていた。 * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） - [[正平 (日本)|正平]]18年（[[1363年]]）に三善貞行により再建。入母屋造三間社、正面千鳥破風、軒唐破風付き、檜皮葺きとする。正面の向拝には丸みを帯びた唐破風を造り、屋根の斜面正面に付けられた三角形の千鳥破風が唐破風の上に位置する。こうした屋根形式は[[室町時代]]の[[神社建築]]としては珍しく、貴重なものとされる。この建築様式は「'''錦織造'''」と呼ばれる複雑な屋根様式の原型であり、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]に建築された神社に多く採用された。また、[[日光東照宮]]にも影響を与えたといわれている。 * 神門 * 祈祷所 * 神輿庫 * 拝殿（割拝殿） * 社務所 * 遙拝所 * [[天誅組]]記念碑 - 参道脇に天誅組記念碑が設置されている。これは幕末当時に甲田村の大庄屋であった[[水郡善之祐]]が[[天誅組の変]]において、天誅組河内勢の中心人物として息子の英太郎とともに決起したことを示すものである。決起から100年後の[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に水郡家の子孫らによって建立された。 == 摂末社 == === 摂社 === * 天神社（重要文化財） - 祭神：[[カグツチ|火産霊神]]、[[大国主]]神、[[えびす|恵比寿神]]。二間社流見世棚造。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]12年（[[1480年]]）建立[http://archives.bunkenkyo.or.jp/national/nisigoori/index.html 文化遺産オンライン]、2009年3月8日閲覧。 * 春日社（重要文化財） - 祭神：[[春日神|春日大明神]]、[[熊野権現|熊野速玉神]]。二間社流見世棚造。文明12年（1480年）頃建立。 === 末社 === * [[金刀比羅宮]] * [[弁才天]]社 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 錦織神社 3棟 ** 本殿 ** 摂社天神社本殿 - 文明12年（1480年）の棟札がある。 ** 摂社春日社本殿 == 例祭 == * 秋期大祭（10月第2土曜日） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[大阪府]][[富田林市]]宮甲田町9-46 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄長野線]][[川西駅 (大阪府)|川西駅]]下車後、徒歩約3分（西へ約200m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html 錦織神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm 「錦織（にしごり）」のページ] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にしきおりしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=draft}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=draft}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=draft}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|12=draft}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|12=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishikiori Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]]

In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|qq=draft}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]]

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chihayaakasaka|qq=}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinguji|qq=}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato River|qq=}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karahafu|qq=}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchū-gumi Incident|qq=}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nagano Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Nishikori Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]]
