{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi|qq=}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chihayaakasaka|qq=}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its {{ill|jinguji|ja|神宮寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jinguji|qq=}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato River|qq=}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and {{ill|karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/karahafu|qq=}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchū-gumi Incident|ja|天誅組の変|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchū-gumi Incident|qq=}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|ja|川西駅 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nagano Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]]

Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]]

Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:富田林市の建築物]] [[Category:富田林市の歴史]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[en:Nishikori Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}}

In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach

A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}}

{{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG|250px]]
Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}}

Main Shrine (center) and auxiliary shrines (sides) | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|qq=}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nishikori Shrine]] [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}}
