Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|qq=|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|qq=|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|qq=|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}}

Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11650558]] Wikidata name: Nishikiori-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]] and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its [[:en:Jingū-ji|jinguji]]. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]], Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]], the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and [[:en:Karahafu|karahafu]]. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]]. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the [[:en:Tenchūgumi incident|Tenchū-gumi Incident]] and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)]] on the [[:en:Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Nagano Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
