Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Konrinji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Rake board|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Q23997371|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji|1=Jingū-ji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Q11457208|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Bargeboard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu|1=Karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident|1=Tenchūgumi incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|1=Kawanishi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Q23997371|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji|1=Jingū-ji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Q11457208|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Bargeboard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu|1=Karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident|1=Tenchūgumi incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|1=Kawanishi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|1=Kawanishi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishikiori-Jinja]][[ja:錦織神社]] {{Otheruses|A Shinto shrine in Tondabayashi, Osaka|Other uses}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nishikiori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nishikiori-Jinja01.JPG | Address = Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture | Latitude = 34 | Latitude_minutes = 29 | Latitude_seconds = 43.2 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude_minutes = 35 | Longitude_seconds = 19.2 | E = E | Country = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = Unknown (estimated mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}) | Main_building = Nishikiori-zukuri | Annual_festival = Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) | location = {{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.495333|N|135.588667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Nishikiori Shrine''' (錦織神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a [[gōsha]], the shrine is locally referred to as "Nishikori," "Nishikoori," or "Nishigori." Along with {{ill|Takesuimori Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takesuimori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}} and {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Migukurumitama Shrine|1=Migukurumitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608777}} in Tondabayashi, it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of Kawachi Province." == Deities == * Primary deities: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The region surrounding the shrine was once called "Nishikori-no-kori" and is believed to have been settled by people from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] who brought advanced textile techniques. Originally known as "Nigori Shrine," it served as the clan shrine of the {{ill|Nishikori clan|ja|錦部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. While the exact date of establishment is unknown, archaeological findings from the 1935 restoration suggest it dates back to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} or earlier, based on the discovery of roof tiles from that era.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/05/09/nishikiorijinja/ Nishikiori Shrine – A Shrine Influencing Nikko Toshogu Design – Tondabayashi City Nishikiori Shrine Access and Goshuin], Retrieved February 14, 2019 The shrine was also called "Nigori Tennō" and "Mizugori Tennō." During the Heian period, the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple {{ill|Konrinji|ja|金輪寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konrinji|1=Q23997371|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997371}} was established as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|WD=Q1074971|lt=jinguji|1=Jingū-ji}}. Due to its location near the {{ill|Ishikawa River (Osaka)|ja|石川 (大阪府)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa River (Osaka)|1=Ishi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585622}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}}, Nishikiori Shrine has long been associated with the "Three Mikomari Shrines of Kawachi Province." Its main hall, rebuilt in 1363 by Mitsuyoshi Sadayuki, is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, following the government-mandated {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, the jinguji temple Konrinji was abolished. In 1872, the shrine was designated a [[gōsha]], and it adopted its current name in 1907 when it became a shrine for government offerings. == Grounds == [[File:Nishikiori-Jinja02.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Torii at the start of the approach
(To the left of this photo is the gate of {{ill|Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|ja|富田林市立川西小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondabayashi Municipal Kawanishi Elementary School|1=Q11457208|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11457208}})]] The shrine is accessed through a large stone torii near the gate of Kawanishi Elementary School. A 150-meter approach leads to the main grounds.[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2523.html Nishikiori Shrine – Tondabayashi City Cultural Properties Division], Retrieved February 14, 2019 * '''Main Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1363. Nishikiori-zukuri style with an intricate roof structure featuring {{ill|chidori-hafu|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chidori-hafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Bargeboard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4881511}} and {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|WD=Q977293|lt=karahafu|1=Karahafu}}. The design inspired later shrine architecture, including {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}. * '''Torii''' * '''Prayer Hall''' * '''Shrine Office''' * '''Memorial Monument''' - Dedicated to the {{ill|Tenchūgumi incident|en|Tenchūgumi incident|WD=Q7699558|lt=Tenchū-gumi Incident|1=Tenchūgumi incident}} and the shrine’s historical ties to the movement. == Sub-shrines == === Auxiliary Shrines === * '''Tenjin Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Ebisu|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. * '''Kasuga Shrine''' (Important Cultural Property) - Dedicated to {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現}} and {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1480. === Associated Shrines === * '''Kotohiragu''' * '''Benzaiten Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === Designated Important Cultural Properties include: * Main Shrine * Tenjin Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) * Kasuga Shrine (Auxiliary Shrine) == festivals == * Autumn Grand Festival (2nd Saturday of October) == Visitor Information == '''Address''': Miyakōda-chō 9-46, Tondabayashi City, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''': Approximately 3 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kawanishi Station (Osaka)|WD=Q6379664|lt=Kawanishi Station (Kintetsu Nagano Line)|1=Kawanishi Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Nishikiori-Jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/tondabayasi-city/m15_02_tonndabayasi_nisigoori.html Nishikiori Shrine – Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] * [http://www.cnet-sc.ne.jp/nisigori/nisigori.htm Nishikiori Shrine Information Page] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto-related navbox}} {{translated page|ja|錦織神社|version=96879511|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:錦織神社]] {{Commons category|Nishikiori-Jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11650558}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
